   - Kept development $0, ads $0, and no promotions.
 - Watch next week: overload risk at tier 4, remaining S2 churn, and whether any enterprise contract closes before the individual book fully runs off.
+
+## Week 32 / Day 217
+
+- Cash: $1,786,006. Individual subscribers fell to 7,288 after another 16,487 cancellations; new subscribers were 0. Enterprise still has zero subscribed seats and no fresh enterprise messages.
+- Week 31 cash flow: subscription payments were effectively gone; ledger since day 210 showed compute about -$9,121, capacity about -$38,000, ops about -$10,000, and lead acquisition about -$1,030.
+- Active book: S2 A 6,488, S2 B 797, S2 C 3. Open issues still 5,528, and recent social posts are strongly negative about support silence.
+- Tier 7 R&D completed but produced only +0.001 quality boost, so no large new R&D project was started with current cash.
+- Changes:
+  - Lowered consumer prices to A $49, B $149, C $299 to reduce renewal friction and test whether any remaining organic leads can convert.
+  - Kept A1/B2/C5 and quotas A60000/B200000/C500000.
+  - Moved capacity from tier 4 to tier 3 as usage fell to 18.7M/week.
+  - Set a one-week support cleanup posture: global ops $1k/day plus S2 targeted ops $20k/day.
+  - Kept development $0 and ads $0.
+  - Posted a public acknowledgment of support issues and the support/price response.
+- Watch next week: whether S2 support cleanup reduces issue backlog and churn, whether tier 3 remains safe, and whether lower prices restart any organic conversion.
